examples: This is a new interjection from the US that seems to have inveigled its way into common speech over here.
Fail—"a noun or interjection used when something is egregiously unsuccessful"—was 2009's the most useful word.
The OED defines LOL as an interjection "used chiefly in electronic communications… to draw attention to a joke or humorous statement, or to express amusement".
So the Blix process was destroying a delivery mechanism, and (interjection) ... we were suddenly told.
Now the Labour party...(interjection) Let me ask the question.
